+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-138
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_02-49-15+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs — added `SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward` enum variant, Ctrl+4 → variant mapping, and handler arm calling `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs — extended `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` and `ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound` to cover Digit4 mapping plus unbound (Ctrl+Shift+4, bare Digit4, Shift-only Digit4)
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs — added `ctrl_4_on_fresh_shell_sets_time_scale_to_max`, `ctrl_4_then_undo_restores_time_scale_to_default`, `repeated_ctrl_4_at_max_is_noop`
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_TASK_2026-05-24_02-39-31+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`:
+  - Added a fourth time-scale variant `SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward` to
+    `EditorKeyCommand` with a doc comment mirroring the existing
+    `ResetTimeScaleDefault` shape.
+  - Extended `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` with one new match arm:
+    `KeyCode::Digit4 => Self::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward` (still gated by
+    the `!ctrl || shift` early-return so Ctrl+Shift+4, bare Digit4, and
+    Shift-only Digit4 remain `None`).
+  - Added one match arm in `EditorShell::handle_key_command`:
+    `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward => self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`.
+  - No changes to `SetTimeScale`, `submit_action`, `set_time_scale`, the
+    `Action` trait surface, the bus's coalesce window, the audit ledger,
+    or any other production behavior.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::key_command_mapping_table_is_exact`:
+  added `assert_eq!(EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(KeyCode::Digit4, true, false),
+  Some(EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward))`.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound`:
+  added three asserts pinning `Ctrl+Shift+4`, bare `Digit4`, and Shift-only
+  `Digit4` all return `None`.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_4_on_fresh_shell_sets_time_scale_to_max`:
+  pins that Ctrl+4 from a fresh shell drives `time_scale().value()` to
+  `TimeScale::MAX`, pushes exactly one stack entry, advances the cursor by
+  one, marks the bus dirty, and records exactly one
+  `AuditEvent::TimeScaleChanged { from: 1.0, to: TimeScale::MAX }` on the
+  editor-shell audit ledger.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_4_then_undo_restores_time_scale_to_default`:
+  pins that Ctrl+Z after Ctrl+4 restores `time_scale().value()` to
+  `TimeScale::DEFAULT`.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::repeated_ctrl_4_at_max_is_noop`:
+  pins that a second Ctrl+4 while already at `TimeScale::MAX` does not push
+  a stack entry, does not advance the cursor, does not change the dirty
+  flag, and does not append a second `TimeScaleChanged` audit event — i.e.
+  the existing `set_time_scale` no-op short-circuit remains authoritative
+  for repeat Ctrl+4.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. The dispatch forbids doc edits.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`: +9 lines of doc + variant,
+  +1 mapping arm, +1 handler arm. No formatting churn outside the additive
+  surface; existing arms unchanged.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`: +4 lines
+  to the positive mapping table, +15 lines to the negative-mapping guard.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`: +118 lines for the three
+  new tests; existing tests untouched.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (pre-edit) → empty (no
+  pre-existing tracked changes).
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` → exit 0 (`Finished` 2.08s).
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` → exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip`
+  → exit 0; 10 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (includes the extended
+  `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` and `ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound`).
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` → exit 0;
+  22 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (includes the three new Ctrl+4 tests:
+  `ctrl_4_on_fresh_shell_sets_time_scale_to_max`,
+  `ctrl_4_then_undo_restores_time_scale_to_default`,
+  `repeated_ctrl_4_at_max_is_noop`).
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0;
+  10 enforcement PASS (`forbidden-dep`, `split-exemption`, `no-utils`,
+  `graph-foundation`, `editor-state-ownership`, `command-bus`,
+  `projection-modules`, `kernel-isolation`, `failure-class`,
+  `snapshot-participate`) + 1 supplementary PASS (snapshot-participate
+  supplementary 3/3).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0; all 7 verification steps passed
+  (fmt-check, arch lints, arch-lint test suite, workspace build,
+  workspace test, doc test, bench --no-run).
+- `git status --short` (post-edit) → exactly the three allowed-edit files
+  (`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`,
+  `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`,
+  `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`) plus the pre-existing
+  untracked TASK packet + its `.meta.json` sidecar. The new EXEC report
+  and sidecar are added on top of this listing during finalization.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only the
+three `MAY edit` files were modified. No forbidden file was touched. No
+new production `Action` impl, no `Action` trait change, no CommandBus
+internal change, no Cargo / dependency / doc / script / workflow edit.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+None. The implementation is one enum variant, one key-mapping arm, one
+handler arm delegating to the existing `set_time_scale` path, plus
+focused tests. The repeat-Ctrl+4 no-op uses the same `set_time_scale`
+short-circuit that Ctrl+0 and Ctrl+2 already rely on, so it inherits
+the verified semantics without introducing a new code path.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: modified (uncommitted) — `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`,
+  `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`,
+  `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_TASK_2026-05-24_02-39-31+0300.md`
+  (the TASK packet authored upstream of this run, with its
+  `.meta.json` sidecar) and this EXEC report (plus its sidecar after
+  finalize).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-138`.
+- Last commit: `6e95f69` chore(dispatch): seed task #27 Ctrl+4 CommandBus time-scale preset.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-138
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-138
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_02-39-31+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_EXEC_*.md
+- GitHub issue #138
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #138: add the smallest Ctrl+4 CommandBus time-scale action in editor-shell. The new binding must map Ctrl+4 to `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward`, and handling that command must call `EditorShell::set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)` so the existing `SetTimeScale` action and existing `CommandBus::submit` path provide undo and no-op behavior. This dispatch is intentionally narrow: one command enum variant, one key mapping, one handler arm, and focused tests only.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `kernel/ecs/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifest, dependency declaration, feature flag, or generated Cargo metadata file.
+- Any editor-shell production source file other than `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Any test file other than `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- Any docs, ADRs, plans, scripts, schemas, workflows, status files, doctrine files, lint configuration, `.gitignore`, generated artifact, persisted log, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet, after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, comment, review, commit, or remote state.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, fixtures, docs, generated artifacts, persisted logs, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, workflow files, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- `EditorKeyCommand` gains exactly one new production command variant named `SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward`.
+- `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(KeyCode::Digit4, true, false)` returns `Some(EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward)`.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command(EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward)` routes by calling `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`.
+- The implementation uses the existing `SetTimeScale` action and the existing `EditorShell::set_time_scale` -> `submit_action` -> `CommandBus::submit` path.
+- Focused tests cover all required behavior:
+  - Digit4 key mapping for Ctrl-without-Shift.
+  - Fresh-shell Ctrl+4 submit changes `TimeScale` from `TimeScale::DEFAULT` to `TimeScale::MAX`.
+  - Repeating Ctrl+4 while already at `TimeScale::MAX` is a no-op: no new stack entry, no cursor movement, no dirty-state change beyond the first submit, and no second `TimeScaleChanged` audit event.
+  - Undo after Ctrl+4 restores `TimeScale` to `TimeScale::DEFAULT`.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet documents the exact files changed, verification commands run, results, and any deviations or halt condition.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only production behavior change is Ctrl+4 max-fast-forward time scale through `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleMaxFastForward`.
+- No new `Action` trait shape, no new production `Action` implementation, no adapter ledger, no adapter authority layer, no `CompoundAction` wrapper, and no CommandBus coalesce-window change is introduced.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command` does not directly mutate `TimeScale`, `World`, resources, audit ledgers, or CommandBus internals for Ctrl+4; it delegates to `set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`.
+- The existing no-op behavior in `EditorShell::set_time_scale` remains authoritative for repeat Ctrl+4. A second Ctrl+4 at `TimeScale::MAX` must not submit another action, grow the undo stack, move the cursor, flip state beyond the first submit, or record another `TimeScaleChanged` audit event.
+- Existing Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Y, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+2, and Ctrl+0 mappings and behavior remain unchanged.
+- Ctrl+Shift+4 is unbound unless the current mapping signature makes that impossible without broader input-surface changes; in that case halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- Bare Digit4 and Shift-only Digit4 remain unbound.
+- The tests are placed only in `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and/or `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- The implementation compiles without touching any forbidden file or adding Cargo dependencies.
+- The final diff contains no preset trio, step-up/step-down commands, UI, menu, toolbar, egui, editor binary, Cargo, editor-actions, editor-state, ECS, architecture-doctrine, or non-time-scale command changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not add Ctrl+1, Ctrl+3, a preset trio, step-up/step-down commands, playback commands, UI, menu items, toolbar buttons, status-bar changes, egui wiring, editor binary event rewrites, or any visible control beyond the existing keyboard command dispatch path.
+- Do not add any non-time-scale command in this dispatch.
+- Do not change the `Action` trait, `CommandBus`, `UndoStack`, action payload semantics, coalescing policy, audit-ledger design, editor-state APIs, ECS resource APIs, or architecture-lint policy.
+- Do not add a new production action for max fast-forward. Ctrl+4 must reuse the existing `SetTimeScale` action via `EditorShell::set_time_scale(TimeScale::MAX)`.
+- Do not move command code out of `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Do not edit `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`; the existing keyboard dispatch into `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` must remain sufficient.
+- Do not broaden tests into GPU, render-path, egui, editor binary, or workspace-wide behavior beyond the required gates.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these commands in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before editing. Document any pre-existing tracked changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` -> expected exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short` after all edits and gates. Expected Executor-created changes are limited to the three allowed edit files plus the single `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-138_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+If a required verification command fails because of behavior inside the allowed files, fix within scope and rerun the failed gate plus any affected focused tests. If a required verification command fails because of unrelated pre-existing work outside the allowed scope, do not fix outside scope; document the failure and halt with the appropriate status described below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- `rge_input::KeyCode::Digit4` is unavailable or cannot be referenced from `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` without editing forbidden files.
